page: Microsoft Azure B8ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an ARM64-based Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The configuration includes 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading, resulting in 1.0 thread per core. Cache specifications feature 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks place the B8ps_v2 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations, though single-core performance is relatively weak. In stress-ng testing, the server achieved a single-core score of 1822.69 and a multi-core score of 14508.5. Geekbench testing yielded a single-core score of 1105.0 and a multi-core score of 6099.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 565.71 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 88.51 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 47.79 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic performance is limited, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 1307.25, which is categorized as weak, despite supporting hardware-accelerated AES.

This burstable configuration is designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization. It delivers average performance for tasks such as static web serving with 10.75 GB/sec throughput, Redis SET operations at 11.04 million operations per second, and software compilation with a Geekbench compiling score of 9506.0. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose applications, development environments, and lightweight databases that do not require continuous maximum CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure B8ps_v2 is an ARM64-based burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz, it features 8 physical cores, 8 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of RAM. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6099.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 14508.5, alongside a weak single-core profile. Memory bandwidth reaches 565.71 GB/sec for cached reads. This burstable instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads with fluctuating resource demands, such as static web serving, Redis databases, software compilation, and general-purpose development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B8ps_v2 is an ARM64 burstable virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, designed for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B8ps_v2 is an ARM64 burstable VM with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Ampere processor, optimized for variable workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered burstable virtual machine offering balanced memory and compute resources for workloads with variable CPU utilization demands.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Burstable CPU allocation suited for handling variable or spikey workloads
Supports Premium Storage for remote disk attachments with no local storage
Delivers average performance for static web serving and Redis operations
